entities.name: CHICKPEA
entities.description: An economical source of vegetable protein for the poor living in semi-arid regions globally.

entities.name: VEGETABLE PROTEIN
entities.description: A nutritional component provided by chickpea.

entities.name: SEMI-ARID REGIONS
entities.description: Global regions where chickpea is primarily grown.

entities.name: CLIMATE CHANGE
entities.description: A global phenomenon causing increased drought and heat stresses.

entities.name: CLIMATE VARIABILITY
entities.description: A phenomenon contributing to the increase in drought and heat stresses.

entities.name: DROUGHT
entities.description: A climate-related stress factor that reduces chickpea yields.

entities.name: HEAT STRESS
entities.description: A climate-related stress factor that reduces chickpea yields.

entities.name: DRY ROOT ROT
entities.description: A disease affecting chickpea crops.

entities.name: COLLAR ROT
entities.description: A disease affecting chickpea crops.

entities.name: YIELD
entities.description: The productivity of the chickpea crop, which can be unstable due to stresses.

entities.name: CROP BREEDING PROGRAMS
entities.description: Programs aimed at developing climate resilient varieties with desired traits.

entities.name: CLIMATE RESILIENT VARIETIES
entities.description: Varieties of crops developed to withstand climate change and variability.

entities.name: GENOMIC RESOURCES
entities.description: Tools and data used for the genetic improvement of complex traits in chickpea.

entities.name: COMPLEX TRAITS
entities.description: Traits in chickpea that are targeted for genetic improvement using genomic resources.

entities.name: QTL-HOTSPOT
entities.description: A genomic region containing multiple quantitative trait loci.

entities.name: QUANTITATIVE TRAIT LOCI
entities.description: Genomic regions associated with specific traits, such as drought tolerance.

entities.name: DROUGHT TOLERANCE
entities.description: The ability of a plant to maintain productivity under drought conditions.

entities.name: ROOT TRAITS
entities.description: Specific physiological traits of roots related to drought tolerance.

entities.name: MARKER-ASSISTED BACKCROSSING
entities.description: A breeding method used to introgress QTL-hotspots for enhancing drought tolerance.

entities.name: POPULAR CULTIVARS
entities.description: Existing chickpea varieties used as a baseline for improvement.

entities.name: DROUGHT-TOLERANT INTROGESSION LINES
entities.description: Lines developed through introgression that exhibit higher yield and drought tolerance.

entities.name: MULTI-PARENT ADVANCED GENERATION INTERCROSS
entities.description: A breeding method used to create populations with large genetic diversity.

entities.name: GENETIC DIVERSITY
entities.description: The total number of genetic characteristics in the genetic makeup of a species.

entities.name: MARKER-ASSISTED RECURRENT SELECTION
entities.description: A breeding method explored for the improvement of yield in chickpea.

entities.name: DIAGNOSTIC MARKERS
entities.description: Genetic markers used to identify specific traits for crop improvement.

entities.name: CANDIDATE GENES
entities.description: Specific genes identified as potentially influencing desired traits.
